The B.S. emphasis in Applied Chemistry fall outside of the classical chemistry degree and instead draw on significant course material from areas relevant to their particular fields. The Environmental Chemistry program provides students with tools to understand the processes governing chemical transformations in soil, air, and water, analyze for key substances in the environment, and make meaningful predictions about the fates of these chemicals. The Forensic Chemistry program involves the identification and quantitation of scientific evidence both in the natural environment and in urban settings, including substances sometimes available in only trace amounts. Please note that the major preparation for both emphases are identical except as identified below.</p>","groupId":"49876f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>Environmental chemistry graduates with the bachelor's degree will be able to pursue advanced degrees in areas such as atmospheric chemistry, geochemistry, toxicology, and environmental science. They will also have access to a range of scientific careers including regulatory agencies, environmental consulting firms, and industries concerned with the environmental impacts and fates of their products. Forensic chemistry graduates will be able to pursue careers in private forensic labs as well as law enforcement and regulatory agencies at many levels, including police and sheriff’s departments, district attorney crime labs, and laboratories of federal agencies including the FBI, DEA, FDA, and many others.</p>","groupId":"4d876f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>This major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time.</p><p> </p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"51876f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>At the time of TAG submission, this major requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA, with no grade lower than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.</p>","groupId":"55876f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"}]
